perturbation.original_text: 1.35 "Conforming Product" means, with respect to a Beacon Optofluidic Machine or Consumable delivered by BLI to Ginkgo under this Agreement, that such Beacon Optofluidic Machine or Consumable meets the Product Warranty at the time of delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.35 "Conforming Product" means, with respect to a Beacon Optofluidic Machine or Consumable delivered by BLI to Ginkgo under this Agreement, that such Beacon Optofluidic Machine or Consumable meets *most of* the Product Warranty at the time of delivery.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of a 'Conforming Product' requires full adherence to the Product Warranty. This modification introduces ambiguity by stating that a product only needs to meet 'most of' the warranty, creating uncertainty as to the required standards for acceptance. This is an In-Text contradiction because throughout the contract, it assumes products are fully conforming, where the new definition may create confusion, especially in Section 5.4.3(a) where failure to comply leads to Remedial Efforts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4.3 Failure to Supply. (a) Remedial Efforts. If, for any reason, including [***], BLI [***] fails to provide Conforming Product or Service that conforms with this Agreement and the Service Level Standards for such Service, in each case in accordance with one or more accepted Purchase Orders for a period of [***] ([***]) consecutive days or more (each, a "Failure to Supply"), BLI shall notify Ginkgo promptly, including details of the reasons for the Failure to Supply and BLI's estimate of when the Failure to Supply shall be corrected. BLI shall [***] minimize any shortage or delay in delivery of Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables or Services to Ginkgo as a result of a Failure to Supply. Within [***] ([***]) days of written notification by BLI to Ginkgo under this Section 5.4.3(a) (Remedial Efforts) of a Failure to Supply, the Parties shall hold a JRC meeting at which BLI's representatives will explain [***] the cause of such Failure to Supply and present BLI's remedial plan to [***] Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables or Services to be supplied to Ginkgo in accordance with this Agreement and the accepted Purchase Orders, which plan [***] (the "Remedial Plan"). BLI shall consider [***] in good faith any reasonable changes proposed by Ginkgo to the Remedial Plan. If the JRC approves the Remedial Plan, BLI will execute such Remedial Plan [***].
perturbation.justification: The original text defines a 'Conforming Product' as fully meeting the Product Warranty. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the product only needs to meet 'most of' the Product Warranty. This creates a direct contradiction with section 5.4.3, which outlines consequences ('Remedial Efforts') for failing to provide 'Conforming Product'. The altered definition makes it unclear what constitutes a failure to supply a 'Conforming Product' under 5.4.3, leading to potential disputes about whether BLI is obligated to initiate remedial efforts.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4.2 Payment. [***], after generating and delivering a report as set forth in Section 2.4.1 (Reporting) or at such other time as may be specified [***], BLI shall issue an invoice to Ginkgo within [***] ([***]) days of Ginkgo receiving such report or, if any costs under BLI's report are disputed pursuant to Section 2.4.1 (Reporting), within [***] ([***]) days of resolution of such dispute, such invoice to be for the amount of costs owed by Ginkgo, if any, to effect the appropriate cost allocation [***] in such [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4.2 Payment. [***], after generating and delivering a report as set forth in Section 2.4.1 (Reporting) or at such other time as may be specified [***], BLI shall issue an invoice to Ginkgo *at its discretion* within [***] ([***]) days of Ginkgo receiving such report or, if any costs under BLI's report are disputed pursuant to Section 2.4.1 (Reporting), within [***] ([***]) days of resolution of such dispute, such invoice to be for the amount of costs owed by Ginkgo, if any, to effect the appropriate cost allocation [***] in such [***].
perturbation.explanation: The original text states BLI *shall* issue an invoice within a set timeframe, making it an obligation. Adding "at its discretion" introduces ambiguity, as BLI can choose whether to issue the invoice or not. This introduces uncertainty in the payment terms and contradicts the assumption of regular invoicing for completed work. This perturbation contradicts in Section 7.10, regarding Late Payments, because if BLI doesn't *have* to issue the invoice, then there's no late payment, or due date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.10 Late Payment. If any payment due is not paid by the due date, BLI may (a) charge interest on any outstanding amount of such payment, accruing as of the original due date, at an annual rate equal to the rate of prime (as reported in The Wall Street Journal, Eastern U.S. Edition) plus [***] percentage points or the maximum rate allowable by Applicable Law, whichever is less.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating that BLI shall issue an invoice 'at its discretion'. This directly contradicts Section 7.10, which assumes timely payments and imposes penalties for late payments. If BLI has the discretion to not issue an invoice, there's no 'due date' for payment, rendering the late payment clause unenforceable and creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Forecasts of Production Purchases. Within [***] ([***]) days following the Effective Date and on or before the first Business Day of each [***] thereafter, Ginkgo shall furnish BLI with a rolling forecast of the quantities of Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software) and Consumables (on a Consumable-by-Consumable basis) that Ginkgo expects to require from BLI under this Agreement during the ensuing [***] ([***]) month period on a [***] basis (each such forecast, a "Rolling Forecast").
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Forecasts of Production Purchases. Within [***] ([***]) days following the Effective Date and on or before the first Business Day of each [***] thereafter, Ginkgo *should endeavor to* furnish BLI with a rolling forecast of the quantities of Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software) and Consumables (on a Consumable-by-Consumable basis) that Ginkgo expects to require from BLI under this Agreement during the ensuing [***] ([***]) month period on a [***] basis (each such forecast, a "Rolling Forecast").
perturbation.explanation: The original language uses 'shall' making the Rolling Forecast a mandatory obligation. Changing it to 'should endeavor to' makes the forecast optional, thus creating ambiguity. If it's optional under 5.1, then it creates an In-Text contradiction with Section 5.3.1, which relies on these forecasts to determine the maximum quantity BLI is obliged to supply. This uncertainty will create enforcement issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3.1 Issuance. Ginkgo shall submit orders for Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables (on a Consumable-by-Consumable basis) and Services pursuant to written purchase orders (each, a "Purchase Order") issued to BLI in a form consistent with this Agreement. Purchase Orders shall be provided by Ginkgo, shall specify (a) the quantity of Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables (on a Consumable-by-Consumable basis) or Services ordered and (b) the requested delivery date or service date and location. The requested delivery dates or service dates set forth in a Purchase Order shall be no earlier than the Lead Time for such delivery or service. BLI shall not be obligated to supply in any [***] (i) a quantity of the [***] in excess of the amount designated in such [***] in the binding portion of the Rolling Forecast or (ii) a quantity of [***] that is greater than [***] percent ([***]%) of the amount designated for such item or service in such [***] in the binding portion of the Rolling Forecast (such amount, the "Maximum Amount"); provided that notwithstanding the provisions set forth in this Section 5.3.1 (Issuance) or Section 5.3.2 (Acceptance and Rejection) to the contrary, BLI shall use [***] to accept and fulfill Purchase Orders for quantities of [***] in excess of the Maximum Amount in any [***].
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'should endeavor to' in Section 5.1 transforms a mandatory obligation for Ginkgo to provide rolling forecasts into an optional one. Section 5.3.1 explicitly states that BLI's obligation to supply goods is capped by the 'binding portion of the Rolling Forecast'. By making the forecast optional, 5.1 creates uncertainty regarding BLI's supply obligations under 5.3.1. Because the binding portion is contingent on the Rolling Forecast being available and definitive (previously mandated by 'shall'), removing this definitiveness leads to an in-text contradiction. Now, the 'Maximum Amount' that BLI is obligated to supply cannot be definitively determined if Ginkgo chooses not to furnish a Rolling Forecast, rendering 5.3.1 potentially unenforceable and creating a strong contradiction.
